metadata.summary: The openIMIS Backend Location reference module.

functions.name: location.migrations.0010_insert_create_region_location_perms.add_rights
documentation: Add create_region_locations_perms to the IMIS Administrator.

functions.name: location.services.get_ancestor_location_filter
documentation:
A generic service that return a Q object that can be used to filter if a model belongs to a location
or any of its children.

```
param ancestor_uuid

UUID of the target location

param location_field

The name of the location field in the filtered model

param levels

The number of location levels to search up. Should not change until location rework.

return

Q object that checks parent locations "levels" levels deep
```

functions.name: location.migrations.0009_add_location_read_right.add_rights
documentation:
All roles that have access to the Insuree also need access to location filtering.
This migration add's location perms to the roles which have access to gql query insuree perms.
Based on SQL Query:
    select * from tblRole tr
    where RoleID in (
        select RoleID from tblRoleRight tr where RightID in (101101)
        group by RoleID)
    and RoleID not in (select RoleID from tblRoleRight tr where RightID in (121901)
        group by RoleID )

classes.name: location.tests.test_graphql.DummyContext
documentation: Just because we need a context to generate.

methods.name: location.tests.test_graphql.LocationGQLTestCase.test_code_locations_district_limited_query
documentation:
This test corresponds to OMT-280 where the Admin with UserDistricts would create a Region which would then
not appear in the resulting list of locations since there are no districts inside the Region yet and certainly
not in the UserDistricts.
The adapted rule is that if the user is allowed to create locations, he should also see all of them.
In this test, the self.test_region etc are not in the admin_dist_token ["R1D1", "R2D1", "R2D2"]

methods.name: location.tests.test_graphql.LocationGQLTestCase.test_no_right_locations_query
documentation: Query with a valid token but not the right to perform this operation, but Location are

methods.name: location.tests.test_graphql.HealthFacilityGQLTestCase._getHFFromAPI
documentation:
Utility method that fetches HF from GraphQL whose code matches the given parameter.
If multiple HF have the same code, this method will fail.

methods.name: location.tests.test_graphql.HealthFacilityGQLTestCase.test_mutation_create_health_facility_minimal_mandatory_fields
documentation:
This method tests that an HF can be created with its minimal mandatory fields.
This method creates an HF by using the GraphQL API, checks that a matching HF
is found in the database through the ORM, asks GraphQL for the newly created HF
and compares the database-fetched HF to the GraphQL-fetched one.

methods.name: location.tests.test_graphql.HealthFacilityGQLTestCase.test_mutation_create_health_facility_error_mandatory_fields
documentation:
This method tests that a health facility cannot be created through the GraphQL API
if any of its mandatory fields is not present.
In this case, the `legal_form` field is missing.

methods.name: location.tests.test_graphql.HealthFacilityGQLTestCase.test_mutation_modify_health_facility
documentation: This method tests if an existing health facility can be modified

functions.name: location.models.cache_location_graph
documentation: Cache the location graph as a dictionary of edges.

functions.name: location.models.extend_allowed_locations
documentation:
Get underlying locations for given location PKs.
If strict is False, also include parents.

functions.name: location.models.update_location_cache

methods.name: location.models.UserDistrict.get_user_districts
documentation:
Retrieve the list of UserDistricts for a user, the locations are prefetched on two levels.
\:param user: InteractiveUser to filter on
\:return: UserDistrict *objects*

methods.name: location.models.UserDistrict.get_user_locations
documentation:
Retrieve the list of Locations in the UserDistricts of a certain user.
\:param user: InteractiveUser to filter on
\:return: Location objects to filter on.
